instructions: Resolve every required input before side effects.
Stop when a required capability is denied, unsupported, or awaiting approval.
Stay within the project or an explicitly approved artifact root.
Preserve the named output contract and report skipped validation honestly.
Do not publish externally, mutate user scope, or rewrite Git history.

behaviorContract.purpose: Deliver a small bounded implementation with focused validation and no unrelated refactoring.

inputs.name: REQUEST
inputs.description: Target implementation request and acceptance criteria.

decisionTable.when: The request is small, bounded, and has a safe execution basis.
The request expands into material design work.
decisionTable.action: Stop and request a plan or explicit direction.

invariants: Validate in proportion to changed behavior.
Do not publish or rewrite Git history.
stopConditions: REQUEST or acceptance criteria are unresolved.
Required permission or capability is denied.
Changes overlap user work and cannot be preserved safely.
The task is no longer small or bounded.

workflowSteps.action: Inspect only the context required for the change.
Run focused validation for changed behavior.
Report changes and any approved adjustments.

deviationPolicy.instructions: Stop before a non-trivial scope, architecture, dependency, or data-model deviation.

validation: Run the narrowest meaningful automated tests or checks.
Inspect the final diff for unrelated changes.
Report any skipped validation and residual risk.

claimBoundary: Behavior-complete machine-readable contract generated from the canonical behavior IR; the Skill contains the same generated contract plus explanatory authored guidance.
